lessons.title: Why scalability matters: vertical vs horizontal scaling
lessons.summary: Scale up vs scale out: when one big box wins, when many small ones do, and the hidden cost of going horizontal.

lessons.title: Load balancers: L4 vs L7, algorithms
lessons.summary: How traffic gets spread across servers: L4 vs L7, round-robin vs least-conn vs consistent hashing, and when each breaks.

lessons.title: Caching basics: write-through, write-back, write-around, eviction
lessons.summary: Pick a write strategy, pick an eviction policy, avoid stampedes: the three knobs that decide if your cache helps or hurts.

lessons.title: Database choice: SQL vs NoSQL, ACID vs BASE
lessons.summary: ACID gives invariants; BASE gives scale. How to pick a database from access patterns, not vibes.

lessons.title: Sharding (partitioning): range, hash, directory
lessons.summary: Range, hash, and directory partitioning: how to split data across nodes without creating hot shards or resharding pain.

lessons.title: Replication: single-leader, multi-leader, leaderless
lessons.summary: Three shapes of write ownership and the trade-offs they bake in.

lessons.title: CDN basics: how it works, push vs pull, edge caching
lessons.summary: Why a fleet of edge caches makes a global site feel local.

lessons.title: CAP theorem and PACELC
lessons.summary: Why you can't have all three under partition — and what 'else' costs on a sunny day.

lessons.title: Consistency models: strong, eventual, causal, RYW, monotonic
lessons.summary: The contract between storage and clients — strong to eventual, with the useful middle ground.

lessons.title: Indexes: B-tree, hash, inverted, secondary
lessons.summary: Four index shapes, what they cost on writes, and when each one earns its keep.

lessons.title: Message queues: Kafka vs RabbitMQ vs SQS — when to use which
lessons.summary: Three brokers, three different bets — log vs router vs managed pipe. How to pick without regretting it in six months.

lessons.title: Pub-sub patterns: fanout, topic, exchange
lessons.summary: Fanout, topic, direct — three routing models that cover most real systems, plus how to design routing keys that age well.

lessons.title: Distributed counters & rate limiting (token / leaky bucket)
lessons.summary: Token vs leaky bucket, sliding windows that actually slide, and how to keep limits accurate across N edge servers.

lessons.title: Probabilistic data structures: Bloom, Count-min, HyperLogLog
lessons.summary: Trade a tiny bounded error for orders-of-magnitude less memory — membership, frequency, and cardinality at scale.

lessons.title: Distributed transactions: 2PC, Saga, Outbox
lessons.summary: Why ACID stops being free across services, and the three patterns that get you back atomicity-shaped behavior.

lessons.title: Idempotency & exactly-once delivery
lessons.summary: Why exactly-once is at-least-once + idempotent processing, and how to design dedup keys that survive retries.

lessons.title: Consensus: Raft & Paxos in plain English
lessons.summary: Leader election, replicated logs, and quorums — how a flaky cluster agrees on one truth.

lessons.title: Distributed time: Lamport, vector clocks, HLC
lessons.summary: Why wall clocks lie and how logical clocks order events safely across unreliable nodes.

lessons.title: Event sourcing & CQRS
lessons.summary: Store the history, not the state — and split commands from queries to scale them independently.

lessons.title: Storage internals: WAL, LSM-tree, B-tree
lessons.summary: How databases turn random writes into sequential ones, and when to pick LSM over B-tree.

lessons.title: CRDTs vs OT for collaborative editing
lessons.summary: Two paths to real-time co-editing: a centralized transform server vs a data structure that merges itself. When each one wins.

lessons.title: Lambda vs Kappa architecture for analytics
lessons.summary: Two pipelines or one? The dual-pipeline batch+stream design vs the replay-everything streaming design, and where each is honest about its costs.

lessons.title: Multi-region strategies: active-active vs active-passive
lessons.summary: RPO, RTO, split-brain, conflict semantics, and the honest trade-offs between paying cross-region latency and tolerating regional sharding.

lessons.title: The hot-key / celebrity-fanout problem
lessons.summary: Why uniform hashing doesn't save you from skew, and the read-side and write-side patterns for surviving virality.

lessons.title: Backpressure & flow control
lessons.summary: Why most overload outages are backpressure failures, and the bounded-queue/credit/pull patterns that turn overload into graceful degradation.

lessons.title: Resilience patterns: circuit breakers, bulkheads, timeouts
lessons.summary: Fail fast, isolate blast radius, propagate deadlines — the three primitives that stop one slow dependency from collapsing the whole graph.

lessons.title: Designing for 10x growth: how to identify bottlenecks early
lessons.summary: How to predict the next three bottlenecks, buy cheap optionality at v1, and avoid scaling rewrites under fire.

lessons.title: Capacity planning with real numbers (back-of-envelope)
lessons.summary: Memorize a small set of constants, multiply through, and turn product specs into QPS, storage, and bandwidth in three minutes.

lessons.title: Approximate algorithms at interview scale
lessons.summary: Bloom, HyperLogLog, Count-Min, Misra-Gries / Space-Saving, and t-digest — what each one estimates, the math intuition, and when to reach for it.

lessons.title: The 12-step interview frame: how to deliver an HLD answer
lessons.summary: A canonical 12-section structure (problem -> FRs -> NFRs -> capacity -> API -> architecture -> data -> components -> scaling -> trade-offs -> DDIA -> rationale) with phrasing cues for each step.

lessons.title: LSM-trees & SSTables: turning random writes into sequential ones
lessons.summary: Memtable, SSTable file format, bloom filters, leveled vs size-tiered compaction, tombstones and MVCC — the whole engine that powers RocksDB, Cassandra, DynamoDB.
